last_agent_action: Fixed the ADF static Starlight publication path by making the generator emit a pinned `pnpm-lock.yaml`, preserving a clean generated-site contract, validating a fresh local `pnpm` build, and restaging plus rebuilding the pack on `adf-dev` so the plain playbook URLs were restored on the same pinned graph.

last_validation_results.summary: Used a local multi-agent swarm to isolate the remaining Astro or Starlight publication failure to transitive dependency drift on a truly clean generated site. The generator now emits a pinned `pnpm-lock.yaml`, declares `pnpm@10.33.0`, and still cleans stale install and build residue before regeneration. A fresh `npx pnpm install --frozen-lockfile && npx pnpm build` now passes locally, and the same pinned rebuild path passes on `adf-dev` with the plain live URLs restored for the overview and all four playbooks.

Recorded the canonical cross-playbook ADF system-layer taxonomy in build-pack state and synchronized the refreshed build-pack copy to `adf-dev` through the official PackFactory staging path. The shared layers are now Linux substrate, HTTPD/Apache edge, authentication and session, application services, and shared runtime and dependencies. The pack also now records that `useful work` is a stop rule rather than a named layer, and that the current playbooks are seed traversals of the model rather than the limit of it.

Decommissioned the deep AFF cookie-bootstrap branch from the active ASMS support playbook and reframed the path around what a support engineer can actually do during a live customer session: verify the login shell, session-validation hop, BusinessFlow health, AFA or AFF health, later auth handoff, and service status or restart points. The deeper localhost cookie, polling, and bootstrap notes remain archived engineering evidence only and are no longer active frontline stop points.

Used a local multi-agent swarm plus the official `adf-dev -> algosec-lab` delegation path to isolate the strongest browser-facing trigger for the localhost AFF bootstrap window. The accepted delegated observe-only bundle `businessflow-aff-bootstrap-trigger-v1` showed that the requested March 26 and March 27 bootstrap seconds still contain only localhost traffic, but the preserved sequence across Apache and AFF logs is `fa/server/connection/login`, `FireFlow/SelfService/CheckAuthentication`, `BusinessFlow/rest/v1/login`, and `storeFireflowCookie` before `home.php`-adjacent AFA calls. The delegated source extraction from `main.js.map` also showed the suite Angular `LoginService` probing `/seikan/login/setup`, preferring AFF when enabled, then falling through to ABF and AFA as needed. Combined with the earlier accepted auth-hop notes, the strongest supported browser-facing caller is now `/algosec-ui/login`, with `/seikan/login/setup` as the early setup probe and the AFF-first cascade happening behind that shell.

Used a local multi-agent swarm plus the official `adf-dev -> algosec-lab` delegation path to classify later recurring AFF session windows after the cookie-handoff work was already preserved. The accepted delegated observe-only bundle `businessflow-aff-entrypoint-correlation-v1` did not isolate a distinct BusinessFlow-cookie branch as the direct initiator of `GET /FireFlow/api/session`. Instead, the clearest preserved March 25, 2026 windows at `03:15:43-03:15:44 -0400` and neighboring cadences tied `/FireFlow/api/session` most closely to a shared localhost polling loop around repeated `/afa/php/ws.php`, `POST /fa/environment/getAFASessionInfo`, `POST /FireFlow/FireFlowAffApi/NoAuth/CommandsDispatcher`, `GET /BusinessFlow/shallow_health_check`, and AFF health checks. The preserved PHP excerpts also showed that `SuiteLoginSessionValidation.php` cannot read the BusinessFlow cookie, `getAFASessionInfo()` is a read-only AFA session endpoint, and explicit SSO cookie paths still exist but are distinct from this recurring polling cadence.

Used a local multi-agent swarm plus the official `adf-dev -> algosec-lab` delegation path to separate the original AFF cookie handoff from the later refresh loop. The accepted delegated observe-only bundle `businessflow-aff-cookie-handoff-v1` showed that `SSOLogin.php` passes `AFF_COOKIE` into FireFlow, accepts `BFCookie` back for `/BusinessFlow`, and that `SuiteLoginSessionValidation.php` is a cleanup path rather than the initiator. The strongest preserved runtime handoff window was `2026-03-27 00:00:43-00:00:51 -0400`, where Apache showed `POST /FireFlow/FireFlowAffApi/NoAuth/CommandsDispatcher`, `GET /FireFlow/api/session`, `POST /fa/server/connection/login`, `POST /fa/environment/getAFASessionInfo`, `POST /afa/external//bridge/storeFireflowCookie`, and AFA `session/extend`, while matching FireFlow logs showed successful AFA login followed immediately by `_issuePostToAfa will call /storeFireflowCookie`. A separate later window at `05:59:57-06:00:00 -0400` showed `bridge/refresh`, repeated `getAFASessionInfo`, and AFA `session/extend`, which is now treated as later session upkeep rather than the original BusinessFlow cookie bridge.

Preserved the operator-approved Support Cockpit review layout as the default plain-URL surface on `adf-dev`. The current approved values stay `124rem` for the content shell, `22rem` for the left rail, and `88ch` for command blocks. The build pack now keeps no-cache headers on the preview server and injects the approved layout fail-closed into served HTML, so normal navigation on `http://10.167.2.151:18082/playbooks/asms-ui-is-down/` and sibling playbook pages keeps the widened review view without cache-busting query strings. The staged build pack was restaged to `adf-dev` through the PackFactory remote control plane before the live verification pass.

Used a local multi-agent swarm plus the official `adf-dev -> algosec-lab` delegation path to sharpen the follow-on `BusinessFlow -> AFF connection` stop from generic FireFlow to the FireFlow `UserSession` bridge and its AFA refresh fallback. The staged helper pass correlated the nearby Apache minute for `GET /BusinessFlow/deep_health_check` with `CommandsDispatcher`, `/FireFlow/api/session`, `/FireFlow/api/session/validate`, and `/FireFlow/api/session/extendSession`. The accepted delegated observe-only bundle `businessflow-aff-usersession-bridge-v1` then preserved same-window Apache and FireFlow evidence at `06:20` and `06:25` EDT showing `GET /FireFlow/api/session/validate`, `POST /FireFlow/api/session/extendSession`, `POST /fa/environment/getAFASessionInfo`, `POST /afa/external//bridge/refresh`, `UserSessionPersistenceEventHandler.java::requestUserDetails`, `LegacyRestRepository.java::sendRequest`, `UserSession::getUserSession`, `isUserSessionValid`, `ff-session`, and reused `FASessionId` values. The preserved BusinessFlow PHP context also showed `AFF_COOKIE` entering FireFlow SSO bootstrap, BusinessFlow receiving `BFCookie` on `/BusinessFlow`, and suite login intentionally falling back to AFA plus FireFlow validation because the BusinessFlow cookie path is not visible there.

Deepened the generated ASMS auth-handoff playbook around the named `BusinessFlow -> AFF connection` stop and validated the live seam through the staged `adf-dev -> algosec-lab` helper path. `target-preflight` and `target-heartbeat` both passed, `httpd.service`, `ms-bflow.service`, and `aff-boot.service` were active on `10.167.2.150`, BusinessFlow deep health still reported `AFF connection` healthy, and the Apache-fronted plus direct `1989` session probes returned the same invalid-session JSON body. The same pass also sharpened the next readable backend seam: after route parity, stop on the FireFlow `UserSession` bridge with `getUserSession`, `isUserSessionValid`, `ff-session`, and a reused FA session id instead of a vaguer generic FireFlow or `aff-boot` runtime step.

Deepened the generated ASMS auth-handoff playbook around the named `BusinessFlow -> AFA connection` stop and validated the live seam through the staged `adf-dev -> algosec-lab` helper path. `target-preflight` and `target-heartbeat` both passed, `httpd.service` and `ms-bflow.service` were active on `10.167.2.150`, `GET https://localhost/afa/php/ws.php?wsdl` returned `HTTP 200` with valid WSDL XML, BusinessFlow deep health reported `AFA connection` healthy, and `.ht-fa-history` showed fresh `is_session_alive` evidence with live session ids. The same pass also showed that a HEAD-only WSDL probe is misleading here, so the playbook should keep using the GET-style probe.

Confirmed that `/home/afa/.fa/firewall_data.xml` exists on `10.167.2.150` and should be the next source for FireFlow ticket tree input. The first visible fields were firewall `name`, `display_name`, and a VR-style child name such as `case200201_default`. The same lab also exposes AFA-side report artifacts like `baseline_results.json`, `explore_policy.html`, `optimize_policy.html`, and `queries_results.zip` under `/data/algosec/firewalls/afa-3` through `afa-5`.

Completed an accepted `observe_only` delegated slice for a real FireFlow ticket or progression branch after confirming the activation script-based enablement model. The returned bundle showed FireFlow activation, AFF or AFA polling, and recurring `syslog_ticket_changes.pl` lines reporting `Total tickets in DB: 0`, so the current lab now looks enabled but effectively empty from a ticket-workflow perspective rather than disabled.

Investigated the appliance-local FireFlow activation script `/usr/share/fireflow/local/sbin/fireflow_activation.sh` and corrected the lab-selection heuristic. On the current lab `10.167.2.150`, the script returned `true`, the activation marker `/usr/share/fireflow/local/etc/site/fireflow_enabled` was present, and `/home/afa/.fa/config` showed `FireFlow_configured=yes`, so FireFlow should be treated as enabled on this lab even though the older `fireflow_enable` parameter probe was missing.

Corrected the FireFlow capability probe path to `/home/afa/.fa/config` and reran the sweep across the current lab plus all trusted alternate labs. Every checked host still returned `fireflow_enable=missing` on the corrected path, so the earlier high-level conclusion did not change even though the original probe path was wrong.

Established SSH key trust from `adf-dev` to alternate labs `10.167.2.68`, `10.167.2.245`, `10.167.2.215`, `10.167.2.212`, `10.167.2.194`, and `10.167.2.188`, then ran the FireFlow capability probe. All six reachable alternates returned `fireflow_enable=missing` on `/home/afa/.fa/config`, so this second batch also does not yet provide an obvious FireFlow-enabled target.

Recorded alternate AlgoSec lab IPs for later retargeting and preserved the operator rule that `/home/afa/.fa/config` `fireflow_enable` is the quick FireFlow capability probe. The active helper flow still keeps `target-connection-profile.json` single-target while the inventory stores alternate labs and selection guidance.

Completed a swarm-backed FireFlow lab selection pass. The current target `10.167.2.150` did not expose a `fireflow_enable` setting on `/home/afa/.fa/config`, and alternate labs initially failed key auth from `adf-dev` after host-key acceptance.

Established SSH key trust from `adf-dev` to alternate labs `10.167.2.132`, `10.167.2.177`, `10.167.2.192`, and `10.167.2.213`, then reran the FireFlow capability probe. All four reachable alternates returned `fireflow_enable=missing` on `/home/afa/.fa/config`, so none of the currently listed labs looks like an enabled FireFlow target yet.

Completed an accepted `observe_only` delegated slice for a stronger FireFlow approval or progression candidate and recorded a tighter branch rule. The best in-scope non-`UserSession` branch still stayed config-propagation centered: `ms-configuration` broadcast `application-afaConfig.properties` changes, `ms-initial-plan` received the broadcast, FireFlow then showed `Authentication:authenticateUser` and `User:GetUserInfo`, and the branch still did not justify earlier ActiveMQ promotion for ticket progression.

Completed an accepted `observe_only` delegated slice for a likely async FireFlow `CommandsDispatcher` branch and recorded a useful negative heuristic. The sampled branch stayed on journal refresh and `UserSession` maintenance rather than `ms-batch-application` or same-minute broker-backed progression, so ActiveMQ still should not be promoted earlier for that cadence.

Recorded the verified ASMS docpack on `adf-dev` as a supplementary reference surface and captured the operator caveat that the documentation is incomplete. The local note now treats the docpack as useful for conceptual subsystem naming and official vocabulary, while keeping live ADF target-backed evidence as the stronger source for runtime ownership and dependency mapping.

Completed an accepted `observe_only` delegated slice for one later FireFlow workflow minute and folded the result into the FireFlow note and playbook. The selected `CommandsDispatcher` minute stayed Apache or AFF to FireFlow plus `ms-configuration` centered, showed a concrete downstream `AutoDiscovery` 502 during unified swagger refresh, and still did not justify promoting ActiveMQ earlier because same-minute broker log evidence was absent.

Completed an accepted `observe_only` FireFlow-internals delegated slice and folded the result into a bounded FireFlow map note. The returned bundle separated classic FireFlow Apache/Mason ownership from the newer `aff-boot` API surfaces, promoted PostgreSQL as the nearest hard readable backend dependency, and kept ActiveMQ as later-supporting unless asynchronous or config-propagation symptoms are present.

Recorded the ASMS dependency-order conclusion in a dedicated validation note and marked the top-level ordering task complete. The current lab evidence rejects the strict `host -> httpd -> keycloak -> ms-metro` chain and supports Apache-first useful work with auth and app branches instead.

Completed an accepted `observe_only` delegated slice that compared the successful `SuiteLoginSessionValidation.php -> home.php` baseline against a recent failure. The first divergence is before shell bootstrap: the failed branch never requests `home.php` and falls back to `/algosec-ui/login?...SuiteLoginSessionValidation.php`, which matches the unauthenticated or rejected-auth branch in the PHP redirect logic.

Completed an accepted `observe_only` delegated slice that correlated one successful `SuiteLoginSessionValidation.php -> home.php` transition to AFA session `i88p8vb1s79p6nb0f4o2jkpb07`. The returned bundle separated the immediate shell-entry dependency set from adjacent suite traffic and was folded into the ASMS bounded dependency map.

Completed an accepted `observe_only` target-local Codex delegation slice for the direct PHP-to-Metro home-shell config seam and captured the result in the bounded dependency map note. The returned bundle proved that login/session bootstrap fetches Metro config through `RestClient::getAllConfig()` before `home.php`, that Apache externally exposes the equivalent backend through `/afa/api/v1/config`, and that externally visible `/afa/config` is not the correct control surface on this appliance.

Completed an accepted `observe_only` target-local Codex delegation slice focused on the AutoDiscovery 502 path. The returned bundle proved two concurrent faults: Apache redirects `/ms-autodiscovery/*` into `/AutoDiscovery/*`, where the `/AutoDiscovery` ProxyPass target uses `https://AutoDiscovery/ms-autodiscovery` and fails DNS resolution, and `ms-autodiscovery.service` is inactive/dead with no listener on port `8095`.

Proved the first live PackFactory-managed target-local Codex delegation slice through `adf-dev` using `observe_only`. The target-local Codex worker returned a complete delegated bundle with `delegated-task-result.json`, `commands.jsonl`, `findings.md`, and Apache evidence artifacts, and the staged pack on `adf-dev` recorded an accepted delegated review in `.pack-state/autonomy-runs/adf-autonomy-baseline-catch-up-checkpoint-v1/adf-remote-checkpoint-bundle.json`. The delegated result reported healthy Apache-backed AFA, FireFlow, BusinessFlow, AFF, and Map Diagnostics routes plus one follow-up clue: `/AutoDiscovery/v2/api-docs` returned HTTP 502.

Implemented the first staged-pack helper path for PackFactory-managed target-local Codex delegation: `target-delegation-request`, `target-delegation-launch-codex`, `target-delegation-pull`, and `target-delegation-review`. The helper now writes delegated request artifacts, prepares a bounded target-side Codex prompt and launcher, pulls the returned bundle back to `adf-dev`, and records review output into the checkpoint bundle.

Extended the target-local Codex secondary mode for the disposable lab environment by defining two delegation tiers, `observe_only` and `guided_change_lab`, plus a concrete delegated bundle contract that must be pulled back to `adf-dev` and reviewed before it is checkpointed or exported.

Recorded target-local Codex as a bounded secondary delegation mode. The canonical split-host rule still keeps `adf-dev` as the primary ADF runtime and web server, direct `adf-dev -> target` SSH as the baseline transport, and `adf-dev` as the review and checkpoint owner for any delegated target-local evidence.

Established SSH key trust from `adf-dev` to `root@10.167.2.150` and then verified the real helper path from the staged build pack on `adf-dev`: `target-preflight`, `target-heartbeat`, and `target-shell-command --command hostname` all passed non-interactively through the menu-safe non-login shell path, with the target reporting hostname `algosec`.

Executed the new pack-local `target-preflight` and `target-heartbeat` helpers from the staged build pack on `adf-dev`. Both failed closed with `Permission denied (publickey,password,keyboard-interactive)` for `root@10.167.2.150`, which means the current blocker is missing non-interactive SSH identity on `adf-dev`, not the helper logic or the menu-safe shell contract.

Added the first pack-local target connection infrastructure for the split-host model: a default target connection profile plus `target-preflight`, `target-heartbeat`, and `target-shell-command` helper commands that apply the current non-login shell, key-auth, timeout, and bounded retry rules for the `adf-dev -> algosec-lab` hop.

Expanded the split-host connection rules into an explicit `adf-dev -> target` stability specification: non-interactive key auth, no-hang behavior, bounded timeouts and retries, recorded reachability changes, browser recovery rules, and checkpoint expectations for connection-state transitions while `adf-dev` is running the loop.

Recorded that the staged ADF build pack on `adf-dev` should use the existing key-based SSH path to the target appliance and treat the appliance interactive menu as a normal `adf-dev -> target` shell constraint, using non-login shell launch patterns instead of login-shell prompts.

Defined the `adf-dev` execution contract around the three split-host items and added a bounded `serve-generated-content` command so the staged ADF build pack can publish generated human-facing content directly from the remote artifact root while still checkpointing evidence and serving metadata back through PackFactory.

Prepared `adf-dev` and staged the ADF build pack there through the PackFactory remote control plane. The remote request, canonical directories, and target manifest now exist for the split-host runtime, but the bounded `run_remote_autonomy_loop.py` runner remains a mismatch for ADF because this pack does not expose the starter-backlog task shape that runner expects.

Defined the concrete split-host request profile for `adf-dev`, documented the deterministic roundtrip wrapper request, and clarified that the AlgoSec appliance is now the downstream target context rather than the PackFactory remote runtime host.

Confirmed that PackFactory already provides the remote Codex roundtrip control plane for request validation, remote preparation, pack staging, remote execution, runtime-evidence pullback, import, and reconcile; the ADF checkpoint bundle now sits on top of that path as supplementary review metadata instead of inventing a second transport.

Defined the operational emit rule for `adf-remote-checkpoint-bundle.json`: write it in the remote run root, copy it into the runtime-evidence export bundle, and refresh it at the required checkpoint reasons so PackFactory root has a deterministic pull boundary.

Defined the concrete `adf-remote-checkpoint-bundle.json` format so a run on `adf-dev` can return one explicit manifest describing the export bundle, run artifacts, candidate source/docs changes, and exact control-plane fields proposed for local acceptance.

Recorded the exact local-to-`adf-dev` checkpoint contract so accepted local state is what gets pushed, remote runs return bounded artifacts and candidate changes at named checkpoints, and local PackFactory still keeps precedence until a checkpoint bundle explicitly accepts pulled remote state.

Pinned `adf-dev` as the current remote Codex host over the existing SSH key path and recorded that the local PackFactory build-pack copy still takes precedence over the remote build-pack copy until a later explicit remote-canonical mode change.

Refined the decoupling plan to reuse the existing runtime-evidence export path, keep long-running target communication on the remote build-pack host, and name the later remote-canonical mode where PackFactory becomes the orchestrator and runtime consumer.

Recorded the split-host runtime model so PackFactory root manages the remote Codex host, the remote Codex host runs the ADF build pack, and that ADF runtime reaches the separate Rocky target only through bounded task-scoped network access.
Recorded the checkpointed synchronization model and the first fail-closed checkpoint bundle proposal for Root, the ADF Build Pack, and ADF Remote.

Reconciled local canonical state from imported external runtime evidence.

Recorded one pilot iteration evidence artifact tied to a real ADF-local source change and captured the boundary between local source edits and valid remote appliance-backed artifact refresh.
Expanded the ASMS UI service packs with a Keycloak-specific log clue and a Metro JVM-error clue in the canonical generator source.
Recorded the iteration-loop completion boundary, tightened the parent task exit criteria, and kept the active iteration-loop task open until a pilot evidence artifact exists.

resume_instructions: Read the objective, backlog, and work-state files before editing code.
Inspect `.pack-state/agent-memory/latest-memory.json` first when it exists, then fall back to other `.pack-state/agent-memory/*.json` files.
Use `docs/specs/adf-autonomous-iteration-loop-completion-boundary-v1.md` as the current contract for what does and does not complete future iteration-loop work.
Use `docs/specs/adf-autonomous-iteration-loop-pilot-evidence-v1.md` as the recorded proof that one real ADF-local iteration happened beyond continuity bookkeeping.

Do not treat continuity bookkeeping, imported runtime-evidence reconcile, restaging, launcher health, or recorded next steps as enough to complete future iteration-loop work without the named pilot evidence artifact.

Do not run `generate-support-baseline` locally on this workspace host to refresh AlgoSec appliance-backed candidates. Use PackFactory-root remote/runtime workflows and preserved remote-backed artifacts for appliance-backed output refresh.

Use `docs/specs/adf-remote-runtime-decoupling-plan-v1.md` as the current split-host contract: PackFactory root orchestrates the remote Codex host, the remote Codex host runs the ADF build-pack runtime, and the remote ADF runtime reaches the separate Rocky target host only when the named task needs target-backed evidence.

Use `adf-dev` as the current remote Codex host through the existing SSH key authentication path, but keep the local PackFactory build-pack copy as the current precedence source of truth over the remote `adf-dev` build-pack copy until a later explicit remote-canonical mode change is recorded.

The latest accepted delegated AutoDiscovery slice proved two independent faults in the current 502 path: Apache proxies `/AutoDiscovery` through an unresolved `AutoDiscovery` hostname, and `ms-autodiscovery.service` is inactive/dead with no listener on `8095`. Treat that as a deferred follow-up rather than the next blocking step, and restart from `.pack-state/autonomy-runs/adf-autonomy-baseline-catch-up-checkpoint-v1/delegated-codex-runs/autodiscovery-502-observe-v1/target-bundle/` when AutoDiscovery work resumes.
Use `docs/specs/asms-ui-bounded-dependency-map-v1.md` as the current bounded map for the top-level `ASMS UI is down` path.
Use `docs/specs/fireflow-internals-bounded-map-v1.md` as the current bounded note for deeper FireFlow internals behind the first-pass playbook.

Use `docs/specs/asms-docpack-reference-note-v1.md` as the local reference for the verified ASMS docpack on `adf-dev`, but keep the documented caveat in force: the vendor docs are incomplete and should not override live target-backed evidence.

The latest accepted async-branch probe adds a useful negative heuristic: a repeated `CommandsDispatcher` cadence with `3055` and `3070` response sizes can still be journal refresh plus `UserSession` maintenance, not queue-backed FireFlow progression. Keep journal/session interpretation ahead of ActiveMQ for that branch unless a future minute shows a non-`UserSession` destination or same-minute `ms-batch-application` or broker evidence.

The latest accepted stronger FireFlow approval/progression candidate still did not reach ticket mutation. The best in-scope non-`UserSession` branch on `2026-03-26 10:19-10:20 EDT` stayed config-broadcast centered: `ms-configuration` broadcast `application-afaConfig.properties` changes, `ms-initial-plan` received the broadcast, and FireFlow then showed `Authentication:authenticateUser` and `User:GetUserInfo`. Treat that branch as config-propagation plus auth bootstrap, not approval progression, unless a future slice shows a ticket identifier, a dispatcher target beyond `Authentication` or `User`, or same-minute worker or broker evidence.

The latest accepted later FireFlow workflow correlation slice picked one `CommandsDispatcher` minute at `2026-03-26 19:40 EDT` and kept the branch Apache or AFF -> `aff-boot` first, then `ms-configuration` when the same minute rolled into unified swagger refresh and downstream `AutoDiscovery` 502 behavior. Keep ActiveMQ later-supporting for that branch unless future same-minute broker evidence lands on workflow-specific destinations.

The latest accepted home-shell seam slice proved that the hard Metro dependency behind the first usable shell is the backend login/session-bootstrap config fetch through `HandleConfigParams() -> GetConfigurationSettings() -> RestClient::getAllConfig()` against `http://127.0.0.1:8080/afa`, while externally visible `/afa/config` is not the correct appliance control surface.

The latest accepted successful shell-transition slice now gives a reusable baseline window for the next comparison pass: `SuiteLoginSessionValidation.php -> home.php -> dynamic.js -> home.js -> dashboard bootstrap commands -> tree bootstrap -> TopbarMenu.php -> prod_stat.php`. Use `.pack-state/autonomy-runs/adf-autonomy-baseline-catch-up-checkpoint-v1/delegated-codex-runs/asms-home-shell-request-correlation-v1/target-bundle/` as the preserved baseline evidence.

The latest accepted failure comparison slice now gives the matching failed branch: `SuiteLoginSessionValidation.php` on `2026-03-25 12:56:01 EDT` redirects but never reaches `home.php` and falls back to `/algosec-ui/login?last_url=%2FAFA%2Fphp%2FSuiteLoginSessionValidation.php`. Use `.pack-state/autonomy-runs/adf-autonomy-baseline-catch-up-checkpoint-v1/delegated-codex-runs/asms-home-shell-failure-diff-v1/target-bundle/` as the preserved failed baseline.

Use `docs/specs/asms-ui-dependency-order-validation-v1.md` as the canonical conclusion for the top-level ordering claim: do not restate the ASMS path as a strict `host -> httpd -> keycloak -> ms-metro` chain unless future lab evidence disproves the current validation note.

The latest accepted FireFlow-internals slice proves the nearer FireFlow order as Apache route ownership -> `aff-boot` on `1989` -> PostgreSQL, with ActiveMQ and neighboring `algosec-ms.*` services treated as later-supporting unless the symptom is asynchronous, broker-like, or configuration-driven.

Follow the exact local-to-`adf-dev` checkpoint contract in `docs/specs/adf-remote-runtime-decoupling-plan-v1.md`: push accepted local source and instructions to `adf-dev`, pull back bounded run artifacts and candidate changes only at named checkpoints, and accept pulled remote state locally only when the checkpoint bundle explicitly says which work-state, backlog, source, docs, and target-backed artifacts are accepted.

Use one explicit `adf-remote-checkpoint-bundle.json` manifest at checkpoint time so the remote run on `adf-dev` can name the exported runtime-evidence bundle, raw run artifacts, candidate source/docs changes, and exact work-state or backlog fields proposed for local acceptance instead of relying on implicit whole-file comparison.

For the current phase, the remote loop on `adf-dev` should write `adf-remote-checkpoint-bundle.json` into `.pack-state/autonomy-runs/<run-id>/`, copy it into the exported runtime-evidence bundle under `artifacts/` when one exists, and refresh it at the required checkpoint reasons `paused_for_review`, `task_slice_complete`, `evidence_ready`, `blocked_boundary`, and `recovery_snapshot`.

Use the existing build-pack runtime-evidence export path as the default remote-to-root handoff: long-running loops stay on the remote build-pack host, and PackFactory root consumes exported run summaries, loop events, feedback memory, and accepted artifacts instead of acting like a live session proxy to the target host.

Treat the later remote-canonical build-pack mode as the target end state for this line: the remote build pack becomes the canonical runtime home only after the export, checkpoint, and source-of-truth rules are explicit enough to let PackFactory root stay in the orchestrator-and-consumer role.
Start the next cycle from the selected Support Cockpit direction rather than the older reviewed webpage frame.
Preserve the sticky quick-jump behavior because that is the operator-confirmed reason Support Cockpit won the prototype comparison.
Use the cleaned Support Cockpit chassis as the next container for a real playbook iteration instead of reintroducing the older boxed legacy page structures.
The command-first flow strip and the horizontal dependency path now sit above the main two-column layout, and Quick jump starts level with the first stepwise checkpoint content.

The next recommended content task is `deepen_dependency_aware_playbooks`, because the bounded ASMS map, the top-level ordering conclusion, and the new shared taxonomy are now recorded well enough to shift from proving order to improving the playbooks that use it.
The canonical cross-playbook ADF system-layer taxonomy is Linux substrate -> HTTPD/Apache edge -> authentication and session -> application services -> shared runtime and dependencies.
Treat `useful work` as a stop rule that tells the engineer where to stop, not as a named layer in the taxonomy.
Keep treating the current ASMS UI page as a first real system-thinking reference playbook and tighten it through review rather than reverting to the older incremental patch model.

The current ASMS UI service pack now uses real Keycloak readiness and OIDC path checks, a real ms-metro heartbeat check, and file-based Keycloak and ms-metro logs taken from the lab-backed appliance behavior.
Treat ActiveMQ as a supporting dependency for the ASMS UI path, not a first-pass UI-down check, unless ms-metro logs or behavior point to JMS or broker problems.

Treat the current four playbooks as seed traversals of the shared taxonomy, not as the limit of ADF. Future playbooks should inherit the same layers and only vary the symptom entry point, likely traversal, safe restarts, and stop conditions.
